evidence_index.text: This is a claim for clinical negligence arising out of a consultation on Saturday 25 May 2013 when the claimant, Mrs Kerry Shaw, saw the defendant, Dr Andrew Stead, a general practitioner, at the Grimsby Area Primary Care Emergency Centre, an out of hours surgery.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: It is alleged that the defendant failed to identify the warning signs of cauda equina syndrome and to refer the claimant for appropriate investigation.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The position here is very straightforward as the GP experts and Dr Stead agree about the standard of care required.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: If a patient consults a general practitioner with acute low back pain, the doctor must take a history and conduct an adequate examination so as to determine whether there are any 'red flags'.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: If there are any red flags the patient must be referred to hospital for further investigation.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The claimant told me that on the Saturday morning she had felt like she needed to pass urine and went to the toilet but found that she could not do so.
evidence_index.speaker: Claimant testimony

evidence_index.text: On her way back to bed, she had wet herself causing a wet patch on the floor, partly on the tiled floor of her en-suite and partly on the carpet of her bedroom.
evidence_index.speaker: Claimant testimony

evidence_index.text: I find that the claimant did have red flag symptoms at the time of the consultation.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: Accordingly I find that the claimant has established that he was in breach of duty in failing to refer her to hospital.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: Mrs Shaw was taken to the surgery by her husband. They had to wait some time to be seen, they estimate between an hour and two hours.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr Stead's note does not record the use of a wheelchair, although the letter of response to the letter of claim suggests that Dr Stead's usual practice was to record if a patient was in a wheelchair.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The claimant's evidence was that she did not lie flat on the examination couch.
evidence_index.speaker: Claimant testimony

evidence_index.text: The claimant next sought medical advice on Monday 27 May.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The A&E department was next door and the claimant was taken there on a trolley, arriving around 15.30.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Later that afternoon she underwent an MRI scan of the lumbar and sacral spine which found a large central disc prolapse at the L3/4 level.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Incomplete cauda equina syndrome was diagnosed and the claimant was taken to Hull Royal Infirmary as an emergency where she underwent surgery.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The neurosurgeons did not agree about this.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Mrs Shaw had told Miss Thompson that afternoon that her legs had gone numb.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The claimant bears the burden of proving her case and must do so on a balance of probabilities.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The evidence of Mr and Mrs Shaw was not perfect. There are aspects on which they were vague, or where inconsistencies could be identified.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

initial_state.chief_complaint: Severe low back pain with radiation down left leg
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient reports being kicked in the back by a child on 23 May 2013, followed by immediate back pain. Pain persisted and worsened, with numbness and tingling in legs, dizziness, and nausea.

focused_exam.back: Tender lower back especially left sacroiliac area
focused_exam.neurological: Unable to perform straight leg raise either leg, reflexes equal and normal, sensation normal

critical_findings: Unable to perform straight leg raise

requestables.name: MRI Lumbar Spine
requestables.description: MRI scan of the lumbar and sacral spine

reveal.result_summary: Large central disc prolapse at L3/4 level

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents to out of hours GP with severe back pain and neurological symptoms

timeline_phases.name: Subsequent Medical Advice
timeline_phases.description: Patient seeks further medical advice due to worsening symptoms

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient in out of hours GP surgery with severe back pain and neurological symptoms
decision_points.prompt: What should be the next step in management?

options.description: Refer to hospital for urgent MRI and neurosurgical evaluation
options.clinical_reasoning: Presence of red flag symptoms indicating possible cauda equina syndrome

options.description: Prescribe painkillers and advise gentle mobilisation
options.clinical_reasoning: Assumed symptoms were due to sciatica without red flags

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant prescribed painkillers and advised gentle mobilisation
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Defendant noted 'no red flags' in the consultation

expected_action_court.description: Referral to hospital should have been made
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Red flag symptoms required urgent referral

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Red flag symptoms were present and should have prompted referral
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Referral to hospital for further investigation

patient_outcome.description: Patient developed incomplete cauda equina syndrome requiring emergency surgery

key_findings: Breach of duty in failing to refer for further investigation
Causation established - earlier referral would have prevented significant disability

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to refer for further investigation
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Referral would have led to earlier diagnosis and treatment

tests_performed.result: Large central disc prolapse at L3/4 level

treatments.outcome: Surgical intervention for cauda equina syndrome

allegations.allegation: Failure to refer for further investigation
